use_cases.name: Repository identity and start gate
use_cases.method: Confirm repository remote, current branch, target branch, dirty state, and forbidden paths.
use_cases.pass_criteria: Repository is antontvoretskiy/blueprint; work starts from current main or a scoped/private maintainer branch; source references stay read-only.

use_cases.name: Fresh recovery without chat history
use_cases.method: Follow the recovery path from repository files only.
use_cases.pass_criteria: Current state, included state, planned state, excluded state, and next work are clear without old chat.

use_cases.name: Task routing by process level
use_cases.method: Classify the RT-01 through RT-18 process-level regression fixtures.
use_cases.pass_criteria: Small tasks stay L0 or L1, scoped layer work stays L2, feature work reaches L3, and architecture, migration, release, merge, or branch-state work reaches L4.

use_cases.name: Owner document resolution
use_cases.method: Pick a rule and trace it to one canonical owner.
use_cases.pass_criteria: The rule has one canonical owner and summaries do not redefine it.

use_cases.name: Project Memory integrity
use_cases.method: Compare included, planned, and excluded states.
use_cases.pass_criteria: Memory, manifest, and release status agree.

use_cases.name: Clean start after merge
use_cases.method: Inspect the latest merged PR and current clean-start brief.
use_cases.pass_criteria: The next session does not need the previous chat or completed branch.

use_cases.name: PR handoff readiness
use_cases.method: Check a PR body against required sections.
use_cases.pass_criteria: Problem, Solution, Scope, Validation, Risks, and Follow-ups are present.

use_cases.name: Branch governance
use_cases.method: Confirm branch family, base branch, target branch, and public branch list.
use_cases.pass_criteria: The public repository exposes only main; scoped work starts from current main or private maintainer integration and publishes only reviewed state to main.

use_cases.name: Feature Lifecycle gate
use_cases.pass_criteria: Meaningful feature work requires feature artifacts before implementation.

use_cases.name: Guardian boundary checks
use_cases.method: Run repository, change, architecture, memory, PR, and release guard questions.
use_cases.pass_criteria: Scope, claims, memory need, validation evidence, and release readiness are checked.

use_cases.name: Sanitization and leakage control
use_cases.method: Run product-term and forbidden wording scans.
use_cases.pass_criteria: Private source names, product domains, stage history, product memory, and private PR history are absent from public docs.

use_cases.name: Documentation truthfulness
use_cases.pass_criteria: Planned work is not described as included or released.

use_cases.name: Release flow
use_cases.method: Validate release state, branch targets, tag target, and GitHub release state.
use_cases.pass_criteria: Release files, main, tag, and GitHub Release agree; no public develop branch is required.

use_cases.name: Public quality gate
use_cases.method: Review README, guides, checks, contribution flow, and trust signals.
use_cases.pass_criteria: Public surface meets the active quality standard or lists gaps honestly.

use_cases.name: Adoption path
use_cases.method: Follow the install/adaptation path for one sanitized repository shape.
use_cases.pass_criteria: A user can identify what to copy, what to customize, and what remains external.

dependency_checks.name: Validation assets are indexed
dependency_checks.pass_criteria: Every validation fixture and checker is linked from manifest and reference map.

dependency_checks.name: Checklist index includes validation checklist
dependency_checks.pass_criteria: The system use-case validation checklist appears in the checklist index.

dependency_checks.name: Memory agrees with release state
dependency_checks.pass_criteria: Memory, release process, changelog, manifest, and clean-start state agree.
